headline: Health Privatization Debate Reignited by 3 Hospital PPP Proposals
context: Three new public-private partnership hospital proposals in 2025 revived debate over healthcare privatization risks.

cards.text: 3 new PPP hospital projects worth RM4.5 billion proposed in Klang Valley alone
cards.sub: Critics warn privatization creep will erode universal public healthcare access

cards.h: Existing PPP hospitals charge patients 15-30% more than fully public facilities
cards.s: Cost premiums mean PPP hospitals serve middle income while B40 remain in overcrowded public wards
cards.d: Existing PPP hospitals charge patients 15-30% more than fully public facilities Cost premiums mean PPP hospitals serve middle income while B40 remain in overcrowded public wards

cards.h: Article 5 of the Federal Constitution guarantees right to life interpreted to include healthcare
cards.s: Legal scholars debate whether privatization conflicts with constitutional health obligations
cards.d: Article 5 of the Federal Constitution guarantees right to life interpreted to include healthcare Legal scholars debate whether privatization conflicts with constitutional health obligations

cards.h: UK's PFI hospital program cost NHS 50% more than conventional public procurement
cards.s: Long-term PPP costs often exceed initial savings due to profit margins and contract terms
cards.d: UK's PFI hospital program cost NHS 50% more than conventional public procurement Long-term PPP costs often exceed initial savings due to profit margins and contract terms

cards.text: Healthcare privatization does not improve efficiency. It redirects public money to private profits.
Like outsourcing school lunches to save money and then wondering why the portions shrink. Efficiency gains flow to the operator, not the students.
cards.sub: Healthcare PPPs redirect public funding through private profit structures without guaranteed service improvements

cards.text: Should Malaysia ban PPP models for essential hospital services in high-demand areas
